title: Delete-vs-Wire decision memo for promotion subsystem
content: BACKGROUND FACTS (measured): 6 promotion modules = 635 LOC, 2 tests = 443 LOC, 1 schema = 82 LOC + 8 exports, 0 runtime/handler callers, 3 bench-layer callers (corpus-bench/safety-bench/holdout-bench import runShadowCycle), benches not wired in package.json scripts, handlers/promotion-orchestrate.ts does not exist, derived_tier field does not exist anywhere in skill-advisor source. OPTION A (Delete): −1,160 to −1,400 LOC, deletes 6 modules + 2 tests + 1 schema + 3 benches; no orphan schema types elsewhere; no registry-schema mutation needed (derived_tier was a phantom premise); risk = re-invention but mitigated by iter-12 12-gate matrix as design recipe. OPTION B (Wire-up): +480 to +800 LOC because iter-12 leak categories (Cat A L2 vector, Cat B variance, Cat C rollback-effectiveness) MUST ship with wiring to avoid false-PASS safety net; creates handlers/promotion-orchestrate.ts (150-250 LOC) + integration test (200-300 LOC) + telemetry (30-50 LOC) + enhancements (100-200 LOC); maintenance debt grows monotonically. OPTION C (Partial flag): +250 to +380 LOC; SKILL_ADVISOR_PROMOTION_MODE=shadow|enforce|disabled with default disabled; risk = shadow-forever failure mode where flag never flips, telemetry on leaky gates is misleading; only viable with hard cutover deadline ≤2 weeks. RECOMMENDATION: Option A. The 12-gate matrix in iter-12 is more valuable than 1,160 LOC of dead code; re-implementation cost is bounded; Option B understates scope by ignoring iter-12 leaks; Option C degenerates to B-with-telemetry-overhead without a deadline. SECONDARY (orphan schema check): schemas/promotion-cycle.ts has 8 exports (PromotionLane, PromotionWeights, PromotionCorpusCase, PromotionPerPromptMatch, ShadowCycleResult, PromotionGateResult, PromotionGateBundleResult, plus *Schema zod versions). All are imported only by lib/promotion/, the 3 benches, and the 2 test files. ZERO orphan types exist outside the promotion subsystem; the schema IS the orphan. Safe to delete entirely under Option A.

label: Delete
schema_implications: delete schemas/promotion-cycle.ts entirely; no derived_tier field exists so no registry mutation
primary_benefit: −1,160 to −1,400 LOC dead-code removal + permanent spec-vs-reality drift elimination
mitigation: iter-12 12-gate matrix preserved as design recipe in research output

label: WireUp
schema_implications: keep schemas/promotion-cycle.ts; possibly add baselineUnknownCount to PromotionGateBundleInput; possibly add audit-artifact schema
primary_risk: installs safety net with iter-12 documented leak categories (Cat A no L2 vector, Cat B no variance, Cat C no rollback-effectiveness, magnitude-blind boolean gates) producing false-PASS verdicts on real regressions; fixing those expands scope to +800 LOC
primary_benefit: 12-gate safety net becomes real after enhancements; telemetry unlocks longitudinal analysis
scope_expansion: iter-12 enhancements MUST ship together, not optional

label: Partial-FeatureFlag
schema_implications: add PromotionMode enum + 15-20 LOC telemetry-only schema
primary_risk: shadow-forever failure mode — no scheduled cutover means flag stays disabled and code remains de-facto dead with telemetry overhead; iter-12 leaks ship as PASS-by-default
primary_benefit: lowest immediate risk; reversible; generates real-world data
viability_condition: requires hard cutover deadline ≤2 weeks; without it degenerates to Option B without teeth

label: Bench scripts are hidden production coupling
package_json_wiring: none — benches not invoked from npm run scripts in mcp_server/
correct_framing: zero runtime/handler callers, three bench-layer callers, bench layer itself unwired into CI
impact_on_options: Option A: delete benches too (cost zero — they bench dead code anyway); Options B/C: must update bench imports if shadow-cycle interface changes

label: derived_tier schema field does not exist
implication: Option A schema-deletion cost is full schemas/promotion-cycle.ts (82 LOC, 8 exports), not a single field; no registry-schema mutation required
likely_origin: confusion with a separate derived_tier field elsewhere (possibly memory tier classification, not skill-advisor promotion)

label: handlers/promotion-orchestrate.ts was never materialized
framing: iter-11 F57 was a forward proposal sketch, not a created file; Option B includes file CREATION not MODIFICATION
scope_implication: slightly increases B's measured size but eliminates sunk-cost partial-build complication

label: No orphan promotion-related schemas exist outside lib/promotion
conclusion: the schema file IS the orphan — safe to delete entirely under Option A
